title: What Happens to Learning When AI Does the Thinking?
angle: Explores cognitive offloading — when we let AI answer for us, what happens to our own thinking muscles?

title: The Quiet Bias: How AI Systems Reflect Our Blind Spots
angle: Examines how AI bias isn't just a data problem — it's a mirror of human psychology and social systems.
audience: general public, professionals using AI tools

title: Presence in the Age of Algorithms: Reclaiming Attention
angle: How algorithmic environments are engineered to fragment attention, and what mindfulness research says about reclaiming it.
audience: professionals, parents, anyone feeling overwhelmed by technology

title: Teaching Machines to Fail Gracefully
angle: What psychology knows about resilience and failure — and what AI systems could learn from it.

title: The Empathy Gap: Why AI Can Simulate But Not Feel
angle: A clear-eyed look at what empathy actually is, why it matters, and why AI can mimic but not hold it.

title: Adaptive Learning Systems: Promise Versus Reality
angle: What the research actually says about personalised AI learning systems — where they work and where they fall short.

title: Decision Fatigue in the Algorithmic Age
angle: We're making more micro-decisions than ever. Explores the psychology of decision fatigue and how AI can help — or make it worse.

title: When AI Becomes a Therapist: Boundaries We Need to Draw
angle: Examining the rise of AI mental health tools through a psychologist's lens — benefits, risks, and ethical lines.
audience: mental health professionals, general public, policymakers

title: Creativity Isn't Computation: What AI Art Teaches Us About Ourselves
angle: Using AI-generated art as a lens to explore what human creativity actually is — and why it still matters.

title: The Habit Machine: Using AI to Build Better Routines
angle: Behaviour change science meets AI tools — a practical look at what actually works for building habits with technology.

title: Trust in the Black Box: Why Explainable AI Matters
angle: Explores the psychology of trust and why 'just trust the algorithm' is a fundamentally human problem.

title: Raising AI-Literate Kids: A Framework for Parents and Teachers
angle: Practical, grounded guide for helping children develop critical thinking about AI without fear or blind trust.
